signin.title: Sign in to your team
signin.lead: Authentication is handled by your identity provider. You'll be redirected, sign in there, and land back here.
signin.guarantee: Your private memory is never shared and never visible in this console — that's a guarantee enforced by the backend, not by configuration.

appError.title: Backend temporarily unreachable
appError.body: The console couldn't load this page. This is usually a brief hiccup — give it a moment and retry. If it persists, the backend may be down.

connector.title: Connect your AI client
connector.lead: Paste these into the assistant's MCP configuration. The client reads and writes shared memory through this endpoint, authenticated as the team.
connector.noSecret: None — public client (PKCE). Leave the secret field empty.
connector.reachBody: Only the shared scopes you see here — global and the project scopes. Each member's private memory is never exposed through this connector.
connector.step1: Add the endpoint to your client's MCP servers.
connector.step2Secret: Authenticate with the client ID and secret.
connector.step2Public: Enter the client ID; leave the secret empty (public client, PKCE).
connector.step3: The assistant reads global first, then the active project scope.

guarantee.title: Private memory stays private
guarantee.body: Every member has a private scope the assistant uses for them alone. It is owned by the member and is never surfaced on this dashboard, in the scope browser, or through the connector above.

assistant.title: Prime your assistant
assistant.lead: Paste this into your Claude or ChatGPT project instructions so the assistant uses kumbuka from the first turn — loading the team's steering knowledge and recording new decisions as you work.
assistant.prompt:
You have access to kumbuka — our team's shared memory — through its MCP connector. Treat it as the source of truth for how this team works, and keep it up to date as we work.

At the start of each session:
- Call memory_load_context to load the team's steering knowledge (decisions, conventions, constraints, glossary, status) and apply it without being told again.

While you work:
- Before answering about our setup, conventions, or past decisions, call memory_recall to check what is already recorded. Prefer recorded team knowledge over assumptions.
- When we settle a decision, agree a convention, set a constraint, define a term, or note a status, call memory_remember to save it. Choose the right type (decision, convention, constraint, open_question, glossary, status) and keep each entry to a single clear statement — not a document.
- If something important is missing or wrong, ask, then offer to remember the corrected answer.

Scopes:
- Shared scopes (global and the project scopes) are visible to the whole team — never put secrets or sensitive personal data there.
- Your private scope is yours alone; use it for personal working notes.

profile.title: Display name
profile.desc: How your name appears next to entries you author in this console.
profile.hint: Your email and login credentials are managed by Keycloak — use the link above to change them.

security.title: Sign-in & credentials
security.desc: Password, two-factor authentication, and passkeys are managed in the Keycloak account console.
security.password_sub: Change your password or set up recovery options.
security.twofa_sub: Authenticator app or one-time codes by email.
security.passkey_sub: Sign in with a hardware key or platform authenticator.

connections.title: Where you're signed in
connections.desc: Every active connection to Kumbuka — this console and any assistant connector. End any you don't recognise; that session's access is revoked immediately.
connections.loadError: Couldn't load your active connections right now.
connections.manageIdp: Manage them in the identity provider

private.title: Yours alone
private.desc: The space the assistant uses with you that nobody else can reach.
private.lockedBody: Your private scope is owned by you. It is never shown in this console — not to you, not to admins — and is never reachable through the connector. The assistant reads and writes it only on your behalf, from your authenticated session.

signout.title: Sign out
signout.desc: End your console session and log out at Keycloak.
